title: William Ginter Riva
content:
{{CharHub|William Ginter Riva|William Ginter Riva}} {{Character |real name = William Ginter Riva{{Ref|IMC}} |image = William Ginter Riva-0.png |species = [[Human]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|USA}} |gender = Male |affiliation = {{Affiliation|STI2}} (formerly)
[[Quentin Beck's Crew]] |movie = ''[[Iron Man (film)|Iron Man]]''
''[[Spider-Man: Far From Home]]''
''[[Spider-Man: No Way Home]]'' (mentioned) |book = ''[[Iron Man Read-Along Storybook and CD]]'' |actor = [[Peter Billingsley]] |status = Alive}} {{Quote|[[Tony Stark]] was able to build [[Arc Reactor|this]] in a [[Ten Rings Base|cave]]! With a box of scraps!"
"Well, I'm sorry. I'm not Tony Stark.|[[Obadiah Stane]] and William Ginter Riva|Iron Man (film)}} '''William Ginter Riva''' is a former [[Stark Industries]] scientist and the creator of the [[Stark Industries Combat Drones]]. In [[2008]], Riva was a member of a science team ordered by [[Obadiah Stane]] to try and recreate the [[Arc Reactor]] technology after [[Tony Stark]] returned to the [[United States of America]] after having been [[Kidnapping of Tony Stark|kidnapped]] by the [[Ten Rings (Organization)|Ten Rings]] in [[Afghanistan]], only to quickly fail at the task and be berated for his failures by his superior, who ultimately went to create the [[Iron Monger Armor]]. By [[2024]], Riva joined forces with [[Quentin Beck]] out of a mutual disdain for Stark and worked with him and other ex-Stark Industries employees to [[Mysterio's Masquerade|wreak havoc]] across [[Europe]], building Beck's reputation as a renowned superhero. However, following Beck's defeat, Riva was instrumental in releasing [[Spider-Man]]'s identity to the world, as he directly sent the altered footage of the [[Battle of London]] to ''[[The Daily Bugle]]'' that incriminated Spider-Man for the attacks. ==Biography== {{MD|William Ginter Riva (Earth-199999)}} ===Recreating the Arc Reactor=== [[File:Obadiah Stane and William Ginter Riva.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Riva starts work on the armor]] {{Quote|Mr. Stane. Sir, we've explored what you've asked us and it seems as though there's a little hiccup.|William Ginter Riva to [[Obadiah Stane]]|Iron Man (film)}} During his tenure as a scientist for [[Stark Industries]], William Ginter Riva developed the [[Stark Industries Combat Drones]].{{Ref|SMFFH}} In [[2008]], Riva was forced by [[Obadiah Stane]] to make a suit for him, similar to the [[:Category:Iron Man Armors|one]] that [[Tony Stark]] made in [[Afghanistan]] while being held in the custody of the [[Ten Rings (Organization)|Ten Rings]] earlier that year.{{Ref|IM}} [[File:Obadiah Stane Deleted Scene.png|{{AltFloat}}|Riva informs [[Obadiah Stane]] about the issues]] With work on the suit on the way, Stane met with Riva and demanded that he and his co-workers "dig deeper" as the stakes couldn't be higher, to which Riva told him that they were still having difficulty with the propulsion system and how to crack the [[Arc Reactor|power]], asking him if he could collaborate with the toroidal fusion group. Stane turned down his request as what they were making was a secret data mass project from which nothing could leave the room, leading him to give an speech about the importance of their current technology, vision, and power over [[Humans|mankind]].{{Ref|IMDS}} [[File:QylJ7joYGkI.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Riva is abruptly yelled at by [[Obadiah Stane]]]] Having little progress on the armored suit matter, Stane attempted to get Riva to make a miniature version of the Arc Reactor, like Stark's. Riva confessed though that he was not of the same caliber of genius as Stark, because he could not reproduce his results. When Stane criticized him for being unable to build what Stark did in a [[Ten Rings Base|cave]] with a box of scraps, Riva replied that he wasn't Stark, much to Stane's ire.{{Ref|IM}} Due to his role in aiding Stane, Riva was fired from Stark Industries.{{Ref|SMFFH}} ===[[Mysterio's Masquerade]]=== ====Elemental Attacks==== [[File:Mysterio Evil Grin.png|{{AltFloat}}|Riva witnesses [[Mysterio]] reveal his illusions]] {{Quote|The integration of my [[B.A.R.F.|illusion tech]] with your [[Stark Industries Combat Drones|weaponized drones]] was brilliant. Powerful illusions, and your damage, worked like a charm. And this is just the beginning."
"Thank you, brother.|[[Quentin Beck]] and William Ginter Riva|Spider-Man: Far From Home}} By [[2024]], Riva had decided to work for [[Quentin Beck]], who was also disregarded for his [[B.A.R.F.|work]] for [[Stark Industries]]. Riva helped to program his battle drones to be equipped with Beck's technology to make the illusions of the [[Elementals]] that were "terrorizing the [[Europe|continent]]". Riva was in charge of managing and maintaining the attack drones equipped with Beck's specialized illusion tech, ranging and calculating the amounts of damage they would inflict in the events directed by Beck. When Beck discovered a drone is missing one projector, Riva dismissed it as a minor inconvenience. However, Beck soon found footage of [[Michelle Jones]] finding the projector and threatened to shoot Riva in the head with one of his drones if the matter was not resolved. Fearful, Riva complied and worked to compensate for the weaker drone and ensure to Beck's story was not discovered by Parker's friends.{{Ref|SMFFH}} ====Exposing Spider-Man's Identity==== [[File:William Ginter Riva (Far From Home).jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Riva commanding all of [[Mysterio]]'s illusions]] The [[Battle of London]] took place and had unfolded as plan in the opening sequence, but everything derailed as [[Spider-Man]] intervened to expose the illusion. [[Mysterio]] called off the illusion, sending the [[Stark Industries Combat Drones]] into blatant hunter-kill mode to go after Spider-Man's friends and defend himself. However, Mysterio ended up killing himself by an accidental misfire from a drone gunshot while trying to kill Spider-Man. Turning to a fallback plan, Riva used recorded footage from the scene with the drones on [[Tower Bridge]]'s walkway, and uploaded it into a computer drive along with Mysterio's illusion technology.{{Ref|SMFFH}} Riva later edited the recordings into a video of altered events in [[London]]. Riva depicted Mysterio as the hero, while Spider-Man was the culprit commanding a drone fleet, before sending the video to the ''[[The Daily Bugle]]'' host [[J. Jonah Jameson]], who then released the altered video to global news outlets which framed Spider-Man as a murderer alongside exposing his secret identity to the entire [[Earth|world]].{{Ref|SMFFH}} As a result of Riva's action, Spider-Man sought [[Doctor Strange]]'s help, which led to a sequence of events ending with a [[Runes of Kof-Kol|spell]] being cast that erased memories and footage of Parker from everyone in the universe.{{Ref|SMNWH}} ==Abilities== *'''Expert Engineer''': While unable to replicate [[Tony Stark]]'s work on [[Arc Reactor]] technology, Riva was the lead engineer on [[Obadiah Stane]]'s project to develop a powerful [[Iron Monger Armor|Suit of Armor]]. He also developed the [[Stark Industries Combat Drones]], very advanced pieces of weaponry that were controlled by Stark's AI [[E.D.I.T.H.]] Once he abandoned [[Stark Industries]] to join [[Quentin Beck]]'s [[Quentin Beck's Crew|Crew]], Riva retrofitted some of the drones with Beck's [[B.A.R.F.|Holographic Technology]], becoming an essential part of their scheme. ==Equipment== *'''Main Console''': A specialty computer designed only for commanding the Drone Fleet and coordinating their illusions. **'''[[E.D.I.T.H.]] Linked Computer Station''': Following [[Quentin Beck]]'s claim to E.D.I.T.H. in [[Prague]], Riva was to uplink a connection to Beck's drone fleet so as to have a larger influence of weaponized illusion capable drones. In so doing, Riva was able to use a large pool of video footage to edit and manipulate how the public to see [[Spider-Man]] as a criminal. **'''[[Stark Industries Combat Drones]]''': Multifaceted assault drones with stealth hover capabilities incorporating Beck's illusion tech, and large surplus ammunition supplies. ==Facilities== *'''[[Stark Industries Headquarters]]''': By the late [[2000s]], Riva was a scientist and engineer co-responsible for the upkeep and redesign of the primary [[Arc Reactor]] powering the company into a miniature design similar to [[Tony Stark]]'s. It was in that same facility that Riva would co-create the [[Iron Monger Armor]] for [[Obadiah Stane]], but was detained by [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] during the [[Duel of Los Angeles]]. *'''[[Quentin Beck's Hideout]]''': Riva and his colleagues used this undisclosed location as their headquarters to orchestrate and rehearse their plans to deceive [[Spider-Man]] and [[Talos|Nick Fury]]. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Allies=== *[[Quentin Beck's Crew]] **[[Mysterio|Quentin Beck/Mysterio]] † - Leader **[[Victoria]] **[[Guterman]] **[[Janice]] **[[Shanice (Stark Industries)|Shanice]] **[[Doug (Quentin Beck's Crew)|Doug]] *''[[The Daily Bugle]]'' **[[J. Jonah Jameson]] - Contact {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Stark Industries]] **[[Iron Man|Tony Stark/Iron Man]] † - Former Employer **[[Iron Monger|Obadiah Stane/Iron Monger]] † - Former Employer *[[Spider-Man|Peter Parker/Spider-Man]] {{Col-end}} ==Appearances== In chronological order: *{{Appearance|IM}} **{{Appearance|IMRASaCD}} *{{Appearance|SMFFH}} *{{Appearance|SMNWH}} (mentioned) ==Behind the Scenes== *William Ginter Riva was named after production crew members of ''[[Iron Man (film)|Iron Man]]'', whose names were "Ginter" and "Riva", while [[Jeff Bridges]] choose the name "William" as he felt that it suited [[Peter Billingsley]]'s character. Billingsley in turn came up with the idea to make his character bald as a sign of solidarity towards Bridges, who shaved his head due his role as [[Obadiah Stane]].[https://www.vulture.com/2019/07/peter-billingsley-on-his-iron-man-and-spider-man-characters.html ‘In a Cave! With a Box of Scraps!’: How an Iconic Marvel Cinematic Insult Came to Be] *According to [[Kevin Feige]], Riva's appearance in ''[[Spider-Man: Far From Home]]'' was because [[Marvel Studios]] thought it would be fun to bring him back as a way to tie-in with other installments of the [[Marvel Cinematic Universe]].[https://www.fandango.com/movie-news/marvels-kevin-feige-on-the-mcu-multiverse-far-from-home-post-credits-scenes-and-the-future-of-spider-man-753812 MARVEL'S KEVIN FEIGE ON THE MCU MULTIVERSE, 'FAR FROM HOME' POST-CREDITS SCENES AND THE FUTURE OF SPIDER-MAN] When [[Mysterio]] was settled as the main antagonist, [[Jon Watts]] watched ''[[Iron Man (film)|Iron Man]]'' again and selected Riva after asking himself who else could be affected by [[Tony Stark]]'s death.[https://www.slashfilm.com/spider-man-far-from-home-spoiler-interview/ ‘Spider-Man: Far From Home’ Spoilers: Director Jon Watts on the Post-Credits Scene, the Song He Wrote for the Film, and More [Interview]] ==References== {{Gallery|William Ginter Riva/Gallery}} {{Wq}} {{Reflist}} [[ru:Уильям Джинтер Рива]] [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Iron Man (film) Characters]] [[Category:Spider-Man: Far From Home Characters]] [[Category:Book Characters]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Blond Hair]] [[Category:Bald Hair]] [[Category:Blue Eyes]] [[Category:Scientists]] [[Category:Criminals]] [[Category:Stark Industries Employees]] [[Category:Quentin Beck's Crew Members]]
